document.title: Modernizing CDS Waste Destruction
document.subtitle: From Paper Logs to Digital Precision — Phase 1 Proposal
document.tagline: One entry. One label. One source of truth.
document.author: IT Director

sections.title: The Problem We Are Solving
sections.summary: The same 12 data fields are entered three times, by three different people, on three different forms.

touch_points.name: Handwritten GMP Bag Label
touch_points.description: Operator writes drug name, batch, quantity, strength, and form by hand at the collection bench.

touch_points.name: Paper Form 0010 at Drum
touch_points.description: Same data manually re-copied from the GMP label onto Form 0010 at the vault drum by hand.
risks: Paper can be lost, damaged, or misfiled

touch_points.name: Manual Spreadsheet Entry
touch_points.description: Same data typed into a spreadsheet by a different person, at a later time, from the paper forms.
risks: Entry is not contemporaneous — ALCOA+ violation risk
No direct link between entry and the physical drum
Discrepancies require rework before drum pickup

sections.callout: This is not a people problem. It is a process problem. And it has a straightforward fix.

sections.title: Current State: How It Works Today
what_is_happening: 3 manual data entry steps across different roles and times
Paper Form 0010 physically attached to or stored in the drum
Spreadsheet updated retrospectively, not at point of collection
No automated timestamp or operator identity on any record
what_it_costs_us: Operator time writing the same data repeatedly
DEA Compliance time reconciling typed vs. handwritten records
Discrepancies that delay drum sealing and pickup scheduling
Potential ALCOA+ findings during regulatory inspection

sections.title: Compliance Risk — ALCOA+
sections.description: An FDA inspector or DEA auditor reviewing ALCOA+ compliance would identify multiple gaps in the current process. Phase 1 closes all of them.

alcoa_assessment.question: Who created the record?
alcoa_assessment.current_state: Initials on a paper form. No system-level identity capture.

alcoa_assessment.question: Was it recorded at the time?
alcoa_assessment.current_state: Spreadsheet entry happens hours or days after the event.

alcoa_assessment.question: Is this the first record?
alcoa_assessment.current_state: The spreadsheet is a copy of a copy of a handwritten note.

alcoa_assessment.question: Is the data correct?
alcoa_assessment.current_state: Transcription from handwriting to Form 0010 to spreadsheet introduces error at each step.

alcoa_assessment.question: Will it last?
alcoa_assessment.current_state: Paper stored in vault. Subject to physical damage or loss.

sections.title: Proposed Solution — Enter Once. Print. Done.
sections.summary: Phase 1 replaces the handwritten GMP label and the paper Form 0010 with a single digital entry at the point of collection.

workflow_steps.action: Operator opens a pre-configured form on a Surface Pro 10 tablet at the collection station
workflow_steps.how: Microsoft Forms — accessible via Edge browser, pinned as the default screen. No login steps during the shift.

workflow_steps.action: Operator selects dropdowns for substance, schedule, drum, area, dosage form. Types only batch number and quantity.
workflow_steps.how: Dropdown lists controlled by DEA Compliance and Supervisors — no free-text errors possible on critical fields.

workflow_steps.action: Operator name and exact timestamp are captured automatically. No typing required.
workflow_steps.how: Microsoft 365 identity — the logged-in Windows account provides name and email. Tamper-proof.

workflow_steps.action: Operator clicks Submit. Data instantly appears in the SharePoint workbook.
workflow_steps.how: DEA Compliance can see every entry in real time from their desk — no waiting for paper forms to be collected.

workflow_steps.action: Label prints automatically at the Honeywell PM43 printer beside the collection station. Operator applies it to the bag.
workflow_steps.how: BarTender Enterprise generates a label with human-readable fields, a Code 128 barcode of the drum number, and a QR code linking to the full digital record.

comparison.before: Handwrite GMP bag label by hand
comparison.after: Label auto-printed by Honeywell PM43 on form submission

comparison.before: Carry bag to vault, search for Form 0010 on drum
comparison.after: Bag is fully labeled before it leaves the collection room

comparison.before: Re-copy 12 fields onto Form 0010 by hand
comparison.after: ELIMINATED — data is already in SharePoint

comparison.before: Start a new paper form when Form 0010 fills up
Collect forms, re-key into spreadsheet (3rd touch)
comparison.after: ELIMINATED — SharePoint workbook is the live record from entry

comparison.before: DEA Compliance reconciles typed vs. handwritten discrepancies
comparison.after: DEA Compliance views live data at any time — no reconciliation needed

comparison.before: No operator ID or timestamp on any record
comparison.after: M365 identity and exact timestamp on every single entry — automatic

comparison.before: Paper Form 0010 physically travels with drum
comparison.after: DEA Compliance prints a formatted drum summary report from SharePoint at drum pickup — on demand

sections.title: Technology Stack
sections.summary: 7 out of 9 technology components are already in place and fully paid for. The only new hardware required is 2 Microsoft Surface Pro 10 tablets and their accessories.

items: Microsoft 365 Suite (Forms, SharePoint, Power Automate, Intune)

sections.title: Data Fields Captured
sections.description: The following 12 fields are captured at the point of collection via the digital form.

fields.source: System clock at time of submission
Controlled list managed by DEA Compliance
Tablet, Capsule, Liquid, Injectable, Patch, Powder
SDF, Packaging Lines 1-3, QC Lab, Vault
Active drum list maintained by DEA Compliance
Expired, Rejected, Production Waste, Damaged, Recalled, Stability Failure
Code 128 barcode of drum number + QR code linking to digital record

items.description: How do we attribute entries? Options: M365 login per shift, operator dropdown, badge scan/PIN. Needs input from DEA Compliance and QA.
options: M365 login per shift — operator logs into tablet at shift start
Operator dropdown — self-selected from a list on the form
Badge scan / PIN — RFID reader or PIN entry before each submission

items.description: What happens if Wi-Fi is down at a collection station? Need a contingency SOP defining the fallback process.
options: Revert to paper SOP for affected shift, back-enter into system
Surface Pro cached form with sync on reconnect

items.description: SOP update and validation protocol need to be initiated. Paper Form 0010 cannot be retired without a change control record.
DEA-mandated retention period for controlled substance destruction records (typically 2 years minimum). SharePoint retention policy must align.
Is this record classified as GxP? If yes, validation burden increases significantly (IQ/OQ/PQ, audit trail, electronic signatures). Clarify classification early.

sections.title: What We Are Asking For
sections.summary: Three manual data entry steps eliminated. Eight weeks. Under $5,000.

action_items.action: Team sign-off to proceed with Phase 1
Controlled substance dropdown list (drug names, strengths, schedule classes)
Confirmation of active drum numbers for SDF and Packaging
Authorization to redeploy Honeywell PM43 units to collection areas
Budget approval for 2x Surface Pro 10 (~$3,720 – $4,540 total)
2–3 operators from SDF and Packaging for UAT and parallel run

sections.scope_note: Phase 1 does not change anything about the DEA Compliance downstream process, drum sealing, or DEA 41 submission. It only changes what happens at the point of collection — using tools we already own.

prototype.description: A working interactive prototype has been built demonstrating the Phase 1 system.

views.name: Collection Entry Form
views.description: Tablet-optimized form with dropdown fields for all controlled data, auto-captured operator ID and timestamp, and a submit button that triggers automatic label printing.

views.name: Live Dashboard
views.description: Real-time SharePoint view for DEA Compliance showing all entries, active drums, operator activity, and zero-reconciliation status.

views.name: Auto-Generated Label
views.description: Printed label with all 12 data fields, Code 128 barcode for drum number, and QR code linking to the full digital record.
